description.summary: A stealth-based killer specializing in poison use, death attacks, and assassin spellcasting.
description.flavor: Assassins are precise killers who combine patience, stealth, poison, and dark arcane magic to eliminate chosen targets.
description.role: Stealth striker with precision damage, death attack, poison safety, and a dedicated Intelligence-based arcane spell list.

requirements.special: Must kill someone as part of the initiation.

description.summary: Deal extra damage when attacking vulnerable targets.
description.fullText: The assassin gains sneak attack damage. This extra damage applies when the target is denied its Dexterity bonus to AC or when the assassin flanks the target, following normal sneak attack rules.

description.summary: Study a victim, then make a sneak attack that can kill or paralyze.
description.fullText: If the assassin studies a victim for 3 rounds and then makes a sneak attack with a melee weapon that successfully deals damage, the sneak attack has the additional effect of possibly either paralyzing or killing the target. The target receives a Fortitude save against DC 10 + assassin level + the assassin's Intelligence modifier. The assassin must make the attack within 3 rounds after study is complete. Paralysis lasts 1d6 rounds plus 1 round per assassin level.

description.summary: Apply poison without risking accidental self-poisoning.
description.fullText: Assassins are trained in the use of poison and never risk accidentally poisoning themselves when applying poison to a blade.

description.summary: Prepare Intelligence-based arcane spells from the assassin spell list.
description.fullText: Beginning at 1st level, the assassin gains the ability to cast a small number of arcane spells. Assassin spellcasting is Intelligence-based and uses the assassin spell list.

description.summary: Gain a scaling bonus on saving throws against poison.
description.fullText: The assassin gains a bonus on saving throws against poison. This bonus improves as assassin level increases.

description.summary: Retain Dexterity bonus to AC when caught flat-footed or attacked by an invisible foe.
description.fullText: Starting at 2nd level, the assassin retains her Dexterity bonus to AC even if caught flat-footed or struck by an invisible attacker.

description.summary: Cannot be flanked except by sufficiently advanced rogues.
description.fullText: At 5th level, the assassin can no longer be flanked except by a rogue whose rogue level is at least four higher than the assassin's character level, following normal improved uncanny dodge rules.

description.summary: Hide while observed when near shadow.
description.fullText: At 8th level, the assassin can use the Hide skill even while being observed, as long as she is within 10 feet of some sort of shadow.

description.summary: A rogue and arcane spellcaster who combines skill, stealth, magic, and precision attacks.
description.flavor: Arcane tricksters use magic to extend their larcenous talents, strike with precision, and manipulate objects from a distance.
description.role: Arcane stealth caster with sneak attack progression, ranged legerdemain, and impromptu sneak attack uses.

requirements.spells: Ability to cast mage hand and at least one arcane spell of 3rd level or higher.

description.summary: Use legerdemain skills at range.
description.fullText: The arcane trickster can use Disable Device, Open Lock, or Sleight of Hand at a range of 30 feet. Working at range increases the normal skill check DC by 5, and the ability can be used a limited number of times per day.

description.summary: Gain additional sneak attack damage.
description.fullText: The arcane trickster gains additional sneak attack damage at higher levels. This stacks with sneak attack damage from other classes.

description.summary: Designate an attack as a sneak attack even without normal sneak attack conditions.
description.fullText: Beginning at 3rd level, the arcane trickster can declare one melee or ranged attack to be a sneak attack before making the attack roll. The target can be no more than 30 feet away if the attack is ranged. The ability can be used a limited number of times per day.

description.summary: A martial arcane prestige class that combines weapon training with spellcasting advancement.
description.flavor: Eldritch knights are warriors who refuse to choose between battlefield prowess and arcane power.
description.role: Full-BAB arcane gish with one lost casting level and bonus combat feats.

requirements.spells: Able to cast 3rd-level arcane spells.

description.summary: Gain fighter bonus feats at specific levels.
description.fullText: The eldritch knight gains a bonus feat at 1st level, 5th level, and 10th level. The feat must be selected from the fighter bonus feat list.

description.summary: Advance an existing arcane spellcasting class at every level after 1st.
description.fullText: At each eldritch knight level after 1st, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if gaining a level in an existing arcane spellcasting class.

description.summary: A dual-progression prestige class advancing both arcane and divine spellcasting.
description.flavor: Mystic theurges pursue mastery across two magical traditions, blending arcane study with divine devotion.
description.role: Dual-progression caster that advances one existing arcane class and one existing divine class at every level.

requirements.spells: Able to cast 2nd-level divine spells and 2nd-level arcane spells.

description.summary: Advance an existing arcane spellcasting class each level.
description.fullText: At each mystic theurge level, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if gaining a level in an existing arcane spellcasting class.

description.summary: Advance an existing divine spellcasting class each level.
description.fullText: At each mystic theurge level, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if gaining a level in an existing divine spellcasting class.

description.summary: An anti-paladin who gains dark divine powers and a fiendish servant.
description.flavor: Blackguards are champions of evil, wielding divine power through corruption and cruelty.
description.role: Melee striker with divine abilities, debuffs, and servant.

description.summary: Radiates evil aura.
description.fullText: A blackguard radiates an aura of evil as a cleric of equal level.

description.summary: A ranged combatant who infuses arrows with magic.
description.flavor: Arcane archers blend archery with arcane power to strike distant foes.
description.role: Ranged striker with magical enhancements.

description.summary: Arrows gain enhancement bonuses.
description.fullText: Arrows fired gain enhancement bonuses as the arcane archer advances.

description.summary: Master of shadows and stealth.
description.flavor: Shadowdancers blend into darkness.
description.role: Stealth specialist.

description.summary: Hide while observed.
description.fullText: Can hide even while observed if near shadows.

description.summary: A spontaneous arcane caster who gradually transforms into a dragon-like being.
description.flavor: Dragon disciples awaken ancient draconic power within themselves.
description.role: Transformation class with natural attacks, ability boosts, breath weapon, and bonus spell slots.

requirements.spells: Ability to cast arcane spells without preparation.

description.summary: Gain bonus spell slots at several levels.
description.fullText: At 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 7th, and 9th level, the dragon disciple gains one bonus spell slot of any spell level the character can already cast. Once chosen, the spell level of the bonus slot cannot be changed.

description.summary: Choose a dragon variety.
description.fullText: At 1st level, the dragon disciple chooses one variety of dragon. This determines the energy type of the breath weapon and the final half-dragon transformation.

description.summary: Gain increasing natural armor.
description.fullText: The dragon disciple's natural armor bonus increases as the class advances.

description.summary: Gain permanent ability score increases.
description.fullText: The dragon disciple gains inherent ability score increases over the course of the class.

description.summary: Gain claw and bite natural attacks.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the dragon disciple gains claw and bite attacks if the character does not already have them. Damage depends on size.

description.summary: Gain a dragon-type breath weapon.
description.fullText: At 3rd level, the dragon disciple gains a breath weapon based on the chosen dragon variety. Damage increases at higher levels.

description.summary: Gain blindsense out to 30 feet.
description.fullText: At 5th level, the dragon disciple gains blindsense out to 30 feet.

description.summary: Grow wings and gain a fly speed.
description.fullText: At 9th level, a Medium or larger dragon disciple grows wings and can fly at twice the character's land speed with average maneuverability.

description.summary: Complete transformation into a half-dragon.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the dragon disciple takes on the half-dragon template and the dragon type.

description.summary: A dwarf defensive specialist who becomes an immovable living wall.
description.flavor: Dwarven defenders are masters of holding ground.
description.role: Defensive tank with defensive stance, trap sense, uncanny dodge, and damage reduction.

description.summary: Enter a powerful defensive stance a limited number of times per day.
description.fullText: The dwarven defender can enter a defensive stance, gaining bonuses to Strength, Constitution, saving throws, and AC while remaining in place. Uses per day increase with class level.

description.summary: Retain Dexterity bonus to AC when caught flat-footed.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the dwarven defender retains Dexterity bonus to AC even when caught flat-footed or attacked by an invisible opponent.

description.summary: Gain a bonus on Reflex saves and AC against traps.
description.fullText: The dwarven defender gains a scaling bonus on Reflex saves made to avoid traps and a dodge bonus to AC against attacks made by traps.

description.summary: Cannot be flanked except by sufficiently advanced rogues.
description.fullText: At 6th level, the dwarven defender can no longer be flanked except by a rogue of sufficiently high level.

description.summary: Gain damage reduction that improves with level.
description.fullText: Starting at 6th level, the dwarven defender gains damage reduction. The amount increases at higher levels.

description.summary: Move slightly while maintaining defensive stance.
description.fullText: At 8th level, the dwarven defender can take a 5-foot step while in defensive stance without ending the stance.

description.summary: Agile melee specialist
description.role: Martial

description.summary: Canny Defense
description.fullText: Canny Defense ability.

description.summary: Precise Strike
description.fullText: Precise Strike ability.

description.summary: Enhanced Mobility
description.fullText: Enhanced Mobility ability.

description.summary: Acrobatic Charge
description.fullText: Acrobatic Charge ability.

description.summary: Improved Reaction
description.fullText: Improved Reaction ability.

description.summary: Elaborate Parry
description.fullText: Elaborate Parry ability.

description.summary: Deflect Arrows
description.fullText: Deflect Arrows ability.

description.summary: A spellcaster-scholar who turns deep knowledge into secrets and magical advancement.
description.flavor: Loremasters pursue knowledge above all else.
description.role: Full spellcasting advancement prestige class with knowledge-based utility, secrets, lore, and language benefits.

feats: Any three metamagic or item creation feats
requirements.spells: Able to cast seven different divination spells, one of which must be 3rd level or higher.

description.summary: Learn special secrets that grant chosen benefits.
description.fullText: At 1st level and every two levels thereafter, the loremaster chooses a secret from the loremaster secrets list.

description.summary: Identify legends and information through accumulated knowledge.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the loremaster gains the lore ability, functioning like bardic knowledge using loremaster level plus Intelligence modifier.

description.summary: Gain additional language knowledge.
description.fullText: At 4th level and again at 8th level, the loremaster gains a bonus language.

description.summary: Identify magic items with superior lore.
description.fullText: At 6th level, the loremaster gains greater lore, allowing knowledge to identify magic items as if using identify.

description.summary: Gain powerful lore equivalent to legend lore or analyze dweomer.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the loremaster gains true lore, allowing deep knowledge effects comparable to legend lore or analyze dweomer as specified by the class ability.

description.summary: Advance an existing spellcasting class at every loremaster level.
description.fullText: At each loremaster level, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if gaining a level in a spellcasting class the character belonged to before entering loremaster.

description.summary: A spellcaster who bonds fiendish skin to gain infernal powers.
description.flavor: Acolytes of the skin pursue power by undergoing a blasphemous ritual that grafts the essence of a fiend to their own flesh.
description.role: Arcane prestige class with partial spellcasting, fiendish resistances, spell-like abilities, and outsider transformation.

requirements.spells: Caster level 5th with spells or spell-like abilities.
requirements.special: Must have made peaceful contact with a summoned evil outsider and must undergo the Ritual of Bonding.

description.summary: Bond fiendish skin to gain armor, Dexterity, and darkvision.
description.fullText: The acolyte bonds fiendish skin to his own. The skin grants +1 natural armor, a +2 inherent bonus to Dexterity, and darkvision out to 60 feet.

description.summary: Use poison as a spell-like ability.
description.fullText: The acolyte can use poison as a spell-like ability. Uses per day improve at 5th level.

description.summary: Advance an existing spellcasting class at even levels.
description.fullText: At each even-numbered acolyte level, the character gains new spells per day and spells known, if applicable, as if gaining a level in an existing spellcasting class.

description.summary: Gain resistance to fire.
description.fullText: The fiendish skin grants resistance to fire 10.

description.summary: Unnerve a foe with a supernatural glare.
description.fullText: The acolyte can use a supernatural glare once per day as a standard action against a creature within 100 feet and line of effect. The target becomes shaken and must save or be stunned for a duration based on hit points.

description.summary: The fiendish skin adapts to the wearer.
description.fullText: The natural armor from wear fiend improves to +2, the acolyte gains a +2 inherent bonus to Constitution, and darkvision extends to 120 feet.

description.summary: Gain resistance to cold.
description.fullText: The acolyte gains resistance to cold 10.

description.summary: Fire rays from the eyes.
description.fullText: Once per day as a standard action, the acolyte projects two fiery rays with a range of 100 feet. Each ray requires a ranged touch attack and deals 8d6 fire damage.

description.summary: Summon a fiend from the bonded skin.
description.fullText: Once per day, the acolyte can summon a babau if the skin is demonic or a chain devil if it is devilish. The summoned creature serves for 1 hour and cannot use innate summoning abilities.

description.summary: Become fully joined with the fiendish skin.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the acolyte's type changes to outsider and he gains damage reduction 10/good. Unlike most outsiders, he can still be raised or resurrected.

description.summary: A spellcaster who studies and summons entities from beyond normal reality.
description.flavor: Alienists pierce the boundaries of sane magic, calling pseudonatural creatures and drawing power from terrifying reaches outside ordinary space and time.
description.role: Full spellcasting summoner with pseudonatural summons, familiar changes, metamagic secrets, and alien transformation.

requirements.spells: Able to cast at least one summoning spell of 3rd level or higher.
requirements.special: Must have made peaceful contact with an alienist or a pseudonatural creature.

description.summary: Advance an existing spellcasting class at every level.
description.fullText: At each alienist level, the character gains new spells per day and spells known, if applicable, as if gaining a level in an existing spellcasting class.

description.summary: Stack alienist levels for familiar benefits.
description.fullText: Alienist levels stack with levels in a class that grants a familiar for determining the familiar's natural armor, Intelligence, and special abilities. This does not grant a familiar if the character lacks one.

description.summary: Summon pseudonatural creatures instead of celestial or fiendish creatures.
description.fullText: Whenever the alienist would use summon monster to summon a celestial or fiendish creature, she instead summons a pseudonatural version of that creature and gives up summoning nonpseudonatural creatures with summon monster.

description.summary: Gain alien resilience at a mental cost.
description.fullText: The alienist gains a +1 insight bonus on saving throws but takes a -2 penalty to Wisdom.

description.summary: Gain metamagic knowledge.
description.fullText: The alienist gains a metamagic feat as a bonus feat at 3rd level and again at 7th level.

description.summary: Gain unnatural conviction while becoming less socially grounded.
description.fullText: The alienist gains 3 hit points, but takes a penalty on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Handle Animal checks made to influence nonpseudonatural creatures.

description.summary: The familiar becomes pseudonatural.
description.fullText: The alienist's familiar, if any, becomes pseudonatural. Newly summoned familiars also possess the pseudonatural template.

description.summary: Gain an extra summon monster slot.
description.fullText: The alienist gains one extra spell slot at her highest spell level. This slot can be used only for a summon monster spell and migrates upward as higher spell levels become available.

description.summary: Mad certainty intensifies.
description.fullText: The alienist gains an additional 3 hit points, while her penalty on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Handle Animal checks made to influence nonpseudonatural creatures worsens.

description.summary: Stop suffering physical aging penalties.
description.fullText: The alienist no longer takes ability score penalties for aging and cannot be magically aged. Existing penalties remain, and bonuses still accrue.

description.summary: Transcend mortality into an alien form.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the alienist's type changes to outsider and she gains damage reduction and resistance reflecting her alien transformation.

reviewNotes: Confirm exact mechanical details for Mad Certainty initial penalty and Alien Transcendence benefits from primary source table/text.

description.summary: Silver energy specialist.
description.role: Arcane caster

description.summary: Enhances force spells.
description.fullText: Force spells gain bonuses.

description.summary: A returned-from-death arcane caster who fuels magic through blood.
description.flavor: Blood magi learn the mystical value of blood after dying and returning to life, carving magic into flesh and turning blood into a conduit for arcane power.
description.role: Arcane prestige class with strong spellcasting advancement and blood-based utility, damage, crafting, and travel abilities.

requirements.spells: Arcane caster level 5th.
requirements.special: The character must have been killed and then returned to life.

description.summary: Advance an existing arcane spellcasting class at most levels.
description.fullText: At each blood magus level except 5th and 10th, the character gains new spells per day and spells known, if applicable, as if gaining a level in an existing arcane spellcasting class.

description.summary: Use blood to empower spellcasting.
description.fullText: The blood magus may substitute a drop of his own blood for material components of 1 gp or less, dealing 1 point of damage to himself and increasing the spell's caster level by 1. Costly material components must still be provided.

description.summary: Reduce effective damage for Concentration checks.
description.fullText: When damage might disrupt casting, concentration, or directing a spell, the blood magus subtracts his class level from the damage for the purpose of determining the Concentration DC only.

description.summary: Automatically stabilize when dying.
description.fullText: The blood magus automatically becomes stable when reduced below 0 hit points, though he still dies at -10 hit points or lower.

description.summary: Inscribe spells into scars.
description.fullText: The blood magus can inscribe spells on his own skin as an alternative to scribing scrolls. The rules, XP costs, and expenses of Scribe Scroll apply, and he can have up to six spell scars at one time.

description.summary: Use death knell once per day.
description.fullText: The blood magus can use death knell as a spell-like ability once per day.

description.summary: Store potion-like magic in blood.
description.fullText: The blood magus can store spells of up to 3rd level in his own blood, functioning like Brew Potion with special restrictions. Stored draughts circulate in his body and are ruined if he dies.

description.summary: Create and empower a blood-linked homunculus.
description.fullText: The blood magus can create a homunculus using his blood without meeting the normal prerequisites, permanently sacrificing 1 hit point. The homunculus advances as the blood magus gains class levels, to a maximum of 6 Hit Dice at 9th level.

description.summary: Add blood-drawing damage to damaging spells.
description.fullText: By taking 3 points of self-inflicted damage while casting, the blood magus can cause a spell that deals hit point damage to deal an extra 1d6 damage to each affected target that has blood or a similar substance.

mechanics.appliesTo: spells that deal hit point damage

description.summary: Gain damage reduction as blood withdraws from wounds.
description.fullText: The blood magus gains damage reduction 1/bludgeoning as his vital fluids avoid injury.

description.summary: Cause a foe's blood to violently awaken.
description.fullText: Once per day, the blood magus can make a melee touch attack to deal 10d10 damage to a creature with blood or a similar substance. If the attempt fails, he can try again later that day.

description.summary: Permanently improve Constitution through blood infusion.
description.fullText: The blood magus prepares and drinks a special distillation of his own blood, gaining a +2 increase to Constitution.

description.summary: Travel through the blood of living creatures.
description.fullText: Once per day as a standard action that does not provoke attacks of opportunity, the blood magus can enter a suitable living creature and pass through blood to another living creature on the same plane. A bloody exit can damage the destination creature.

mechanics.bloodyExitSave: Fortitude negates or reduces per source
mechanics.selfStunRisk: DC 15 Fortitude or stunned 1 round after bloody exit

description.summary: A spellcaster who transforms into an elemental being through mastery of one energy type.
description.flavor: Elemental savants devote themselves to one of the classic elements, reshaping their magic and bodies until they transcend mortal form.
description.role: Energy-specialized caster with strong elemental defenses and near-full spellcasting advancement.

feats: Energy Substitution (acid, cold, electricity, or fire)
requirements.spells: Able to cast at least three spells sharing the acid, cold, electricity, or fire descriptor, one of which must be at least 3rd level.
requirements.special: Must have made peaceful contact with an elemental or an outsider with an elemental subtype.

description.summary: Advance an existing spellcasting class at all levels except 5th and 10th.
description.fullText: At each elemental savant level except 5th and 10th, the character gains new spells per day and spells known, if applicable, as if gaining a level in an existing spellcasting class.

description.summary: Choose an elemental specialty tied to an energy type.
description.fullText: At 1st level, the elemental savant chooses air/electricity, earth/acid, fire/fire, or water/cold. This choice must match the Energy Substitution feat used to qualify. Spells that deal energy damage change to the chosen energy type and descriptor.

description.summary: Gain resistance to the energy type tied to the chosen element.
description.fullText: The elemental savant gains resistance against the energy type associated with the chosen elemental specialty. The resistance improves at higher levels.

description.summary: Become immune to sleep effects.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the elemental savant gains immunity to sleep effects.

description.summary: Improve caster level checks to overcome spell resistance with chosen energy spells.
description.fullText: When casting spells using the energy type associated with the chosen element, the elemental savant gains a competence bonus on caster level checks to overcome spell resistance.

mechanics.appliesTo: caster level checks to overcome spell resistance with chosen energy spells
caster level checks to overcome spell resistance with chosen energy spells

description.summary: Increase save DCs for spells with the chosen energy descriptor.
description.fullText: The save DC for spells with the energy descriptor associated with the elemental savant's chosen element increases at 5th level and again at 10th level.

mechanics.appliesTo: spell save DCs with chosen energy descriptor
spell save DCs with chosen energy descriptor

description.summary: Gain darkvision.
description.fullText: At 6th level, the elemental savant gains darkvision out to 60 feet.

description.summary: Become immune to paralysis and poison.
description.fullText: At 9th level, the elemental savant gains immunity to paralysis and poison.

description.summary: Become an elemental creature.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the elemental savant's type changes to elemental. The savant no longer needs to eat, sleep, or breathe, gains immunity to stunning, and is no longer subject to extra damage from critical hits or flanking. The savant gains appropriate movement modes, natural attacks, special attacks, and special qualities of a Medium elemental of the chosen specialty, with the listed save DC adjustment for elemental attack forms.

description.summary: Become immune to the chosen energy type.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the elemental savant gains immunity to the energy type associated with the chosen elemental specialty.

description.summary: An arcane caster who manipulates luck, probability, and fate.
description.flavor: Fatespinners glimpse the hidden structure of chance and bend fortune for themselves or against their enemies.
description.role: Arcane caster advancement class with save DC boosts, rerolls, stabilization, and saving throw manipulation.

requirements.spells: Able to cast 4th-level arcane spells, including at least one divination spell of 1st level or higher.
requirements.special: Must have avoided death or severe calamity through the machinations of fate.

description.summary: Advance an existing spellcasting class at levels 1 through 4.
description.fullText: At each fatespinner level except 5th, the character gains new spells per day and spells known, if applicable, as if gaining a level in an existing spellcasting class.

description.summary: Spend daily spin points to increase spell save DCs.
description.fullText: Each day, the fatespinner has a number of spin points equal to class level. As a free action, the fatespinner can spend spin to increase the save DC of a spell being cast on a point-for-point basis. Spin is replenished when the character regains spells.

description.summary: Force another creature to reroll a just-made roll.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, once per day as an immediate action, the fatespinner can force another creature in line of sight to reroll a roll it has just made. The creature must keep the reroll result.

description.summary: Spend spin points on the fatespinner's own checks, attacks, or saves.
description.fullText: At 3rd level, the fatespinner can spend spin to add a point-for-point bonus to a skill check, attack roll, or saving throw he attempts. The spin must be applied before the roll and comes from the same daily spin pool.

description.summary: Automatically stabilize once per day when dying.
description.fullText: At 4th level, once per day, the first time the fatespinner must make a check to become stable when dying, the check automatically succeeds.

description.summary: Reroll one of the fatespinner's own rolls once per day.
description.fullText: At 4th level, once per day, the fatespinner can reroll one roll he has just made. He must keep the reroll result even if it is worse.

description.summary: Apply a major bonus or penalty to a target's next saving throw.
description.fullText: At 5th level, once per day as a free action, the fatespinner selects a visible target within 30 feet with Hit Dice no greater than his own. The target receives either a +10 bonus or a -10 penalty on its next saving throw, as chosen by the fatespinner. The effect lasts 1 round.

description.summary: An arcane caster who encodes spells into diagrams, glyphs, and perfect magical geometry.
description.flavor: Geometers study the hidden diagrams behind magic, reducing spells to sigils and precise arcane forms.
description.role: Full arcane casting progression class focused on spellglyphs, glyph spells, and magical writing traps.

requirements.spells: Able to prepare and cast 3rd-level arcane spells.

description.summary: Advance an existing arcane spellcasting class at every level.
description.fullText: At each geometer level, the character gains new spells per day and spells known, if applicable, as if gaining a level in an existing arcane spellcasting class.

description.summary: Add glyph of warding to the geometer's spellbook as an arcane spell.
description.fullText: At 1st level, the geometer adds glyph of warding to his spellbook as a 3rd-level arcane spell and can prepare and cast it like any other spell he knows.

description.summary: Use a prepared diagram in place of verbal and material spell components.
description.fullText: A spellglyph is an arcane diagram that can substitute for a specific prepared spell's verbal and material components, if any. When used, the spell is treated as if affected by Silent Spell, and the spellglyph replaces normal material components other than foci. Preparing a spellglyph takes 1 hour and rare inks costing 25 gp per spell level, plus the cost of expensive material components if applicable.

description.summary: Reduce new spellbook page requirements and make the spellbook harder for others to use.
description.fullText: At 2nd level and higher, every spell the geometer learns from that point forward requires only a single page in his spellbook. It still takes 24 hours and 100 gp per page to scribe a spell. Non-geometers have the Spellcraft DC to decipher or prepare spells from the geometer's spellbook increased by 5.

description.summary: Find magical writing traps as a rogue and gain a caster-level bonus on such Search checks.
description.fullText: At 3rd level, the geometer can use Search to find magical traps based on runes, glyphs, sigils, symbols, or other writings as a rogue can. He gains a bonus equal to caster level on Search checks to find such traps, and coming within 10 feet allows a Search check as if actively searching.

description.summary: Temporarily suppress magical wards based on written symbols.
description.fullText: At 4th level, as a standard action, the geometer can attempt to pass a magical sigil, rune, glyph, or written ward he can see. He makes a level check against DC 6 + the sigil creator's caster level. On success, the device is suppressed while he maintains concentration.

description.summary: Increase caster level when casting through a spellglyph.
description.fullText: At 5th level, when the geometer casts a spell using a spellglyph, his caster level is treated as 1 higher for that spell.

description.summary: Add greater glyph of warding to the geometer's spellbook as an arcane spell.
description.fullText: At 5th level, the geometer adds greater glyph of warding to his spellbook as a 6th-level arcane spell and can prepare and cast it like any other spell he knows.

description.summary: An arcane warrior who consumes starmetal and slowly transforms into a living green starmetal construct.
description.flavor: Green Star adepts seek fallen starmetal from the comet Alhazarde, trading flesh for durability, power, and unnatural immortality.
description.role: Durable arcane gish with partial casting, scaling damage reduction, physical transformation, and construct-like end state.

requirements.spells: Arcane caster level 1st.
requirements.special: Must acquire at least 2 ounces of starmetal, powder it, and consume it in a specially prepared infusion requiring arcane reagents costing 1,000 gp and one week to prepare.

description.summary: Gain scaling damage reduction overcome by adamantine.
description.fullText: The Green Star adept's starmetal-infused flesh grants damage reduction 1/adamantine at 1st level. The damage reduction improves by 1 each class level, reaching damage reduction 10/adamantine at 10th level.

description.summary: Add Green Star adept level to an arcane caster level for effective caster level.
description.fullText: A Green Star adept adds class level to the caster level of one arcane spellcasting class to determine effective caster level, even though spells per day advance only at even-numbered levels.

description.summary: Require additional starmetal rituals to gain later class features.
description.fullText: After 1st level, the Green Star adept does not gain special class features for a new class level until completing a 24-hour ritual requiring 1 pound of starmetal and 1,000 gp in arcane reagents and materials. Base attack bonus, saving throws, skills, feats, and ability score increases are not delayed by this ritual.

description.summary: Gain Strength and natural armor while losing Dexterity as the body becomes starmetal.
description.fullText: The Green Star adept's body becomes denser and stronger. Strength and natural armor increase at 1st, 4th, 7th, and 10th levels, while Dexterity is reduced at 1st, 7th, and 10th level to a minimum of 3.

description.summary: Gain a slam attack.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the Green Star adept gains a slam attack dealing bludgeoning damage equal to a club sized for the character, plus 1-1/2 times Strength modifier.

description.summary: Gain a scaling bonus on saves against several biological and necromantic effects.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the Green Star adept gains a +2 bonus on saves against poison, sleep effects, paralysis, stunning, disease, death effects, and necromancy effects. This bonus improves at 5th and 8th level.

description.summary: Gain a chance to negate critical hits and sneak attacks.
description.fullText: At 3rd level and higher, the Green Star adept gains fortification. When a critical hit or sneak attack is scored, there is a chance that the extra effect is negated and damage is rolled normally. The chance improves at 6th and 9th level.

description.summary: Gain darkvision and low-light vision.
description.fullText: At 4th level, the Green Star adept gains darkvision out to 60 feet and low-light vision.

description.summary: No longer need to breathe, eat, or sleep and ignore fatigue and exhaustion.
description.fullText: At 7th level, the Green Star adept no longer needs to breathe, eat, or sleep, gains immunity to inhaled poisons, drowning, suffocation, and sleep effects, and is no longer subject to fatigue or exhaustion.

description.summary: Complete the transformation into a construct-like starmetal being.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the Green Star adept's type changes to construct. The adept loses Constitution, gains size-based bonus hit points, keeps a mind and does not gain normal construct immunity to mind-affecting effects, and gains multiple immunities and construct-like traits described by the class.

description.summary: Repair damage during rest.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the Green Star adept repairs 1 point of damage per hour of rest as long as he has at least 1 hit point. This does not regrow or reattach lost body parts.

mechanics.repairRate: 1 hit point per hour of rest

description.summary: Advance an existing arcane spellcasting class at even-numbered levels.
description.fullText: At every even-numbered Green Star adept level, the character gains new spells per day and spells known, if applicable, as if gaining a level in an existing arcane spellcasting class.

description.summary: A guild wizard who draws on the Arcane Order's shared Spellpool.
description.flavor: Mages of the Arcane Order gain power through disciplined study, professional ties, and access to the Order's shared magical resources.
description.role: Full arcane casting progression prestige class with Spellpool access, bonus metamagic, language, and spellbook benefits.

requirements.spells: Able to prepare and cast 2nd-level arcane spells.
special: Prospective members must pay an initiation fee of 750 gp.

description.summary: Advance an existing arcane spellcasting class at every level.
description.fullText: At each mage of the Arcane Order level, the character gains new spells per day and spells known, if applicable, as if gaining a level in an existing arcane spellcasting class.

description.summary: Gain Arcane Order membership benefits and obligations.
description.fullText: The mage is a guild member of the Arcane Order, paying dues and accepting duties in exchange for access to lodging, library resources, laboratories, job boards, professional contacts, and the Spellpool while in good standing.

description.summary: Call spells from the Arcane Order's shared spell reservoir.
description.fullText: Using a guild focus, the mage can call spells from the Spellpool. Spellpool I allows 1st–3rd level spells, Spellpool II allows 4th–6th level spells, and Spellpool III allows 7th–9th level spells. Calling a spell requires an open slot and a full-round action; the spell must be repaid as Spellpool debt within the allowed time.

description.summary: Gain a bonus metamagic feat.
description.fullText: At 2nd and 9th level, the mage gains a metamagic feat as a bonus feat and must still meet the feat's prerequisites.

description.summary: Learn an additional language from Arcane Order resources.
description.fullText: At 3rd and 6th level, access to the Arcane Order's library and resources allows the mage to learn a new language.

description.summary: Copy a spell from another wizard's spellbook without a Spellcraft check.
description.fullText: At 5th and 8th level, a fellow wizard allows the mage to copy a spell from a spellbook, chosen by the player and subject to DM approval. No Spellcraft check is needed, though specialist wizards still cannot learn spells from prohibited schools. Spellcasters without spellbooks gain no benefit.

description.summary: Become a governing member of the Arcane Order.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the mage becomes a regent of the Arcane Order, gaining a +2 competence bonus on Charisma-based interaction checks with lower-level members and participating in the Council of Regents.

description.summary: An arcane manipulator who controls thoughts, emotions, and wills.
description.flavor: Mindbenders seek mastery over the thoughts and dreams of others, using both magic and mundane manipulation.
description.role: Partial arcane casting progression prestige class with telepathy, charm, suggestion, mind-reading, and domination abilities.

requirements.spells: Able to cast charm person, use charm person as a spell-like ability, or use the charm invocation.

description.summary: Advance an existing arcane spellcasting class at odd-numbered levels.
description.fullText: At each odd-numbered mindbender level, the character gains new spells per day and spells known, if applicable, as if gaining a level in an existing arcane spellcasting class.

description.summary: Communicate telepathically with nearby creatures that have language.
description.fullText: At 1st level, the mindbender can communicate telepathically with any creature within 100 feet that has a language.

description.summary: Gain a competence bonus on manipulation and insight skills.
description.fullText: Beginning at 2nd level, the mindbender adds one-half class level as a competence bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Sense Motive checks.

description.summary: Use a suggestion-like ability on a living creature.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the mindbender can influence a Large or smaller living creature within 100 feet as suggestion, with duration 5 hours plus 1 hour per class level and a Will save to negate. Uses per day increase at 5th and 8th level.

description.summary: Read the surface thoughts of a visible living creature.
description.fullText: At 3rd level, the mindbender can read the surface thoughts of a visible living creature within 100 feet. The ability is mind-affecting, requires a standard action, allows a Will save, and lasts up to 10 minutes with concentration. Uses improve at 7th level.

description.summary: Maintain permanent charm monster effects on a limited number of creatures.
description.fullText: At 4th level, the mindbender can charm a single Large or smaller living creature within 100 feet as charm monster. The duration is permanent, but the number of simultaneous charms is limited and improves with level.

description.summary: Increase caster level for enchantment spells.
description.fullText: At 6th level, the mindbender adds +2 to caster level when casting enchantment spells. This improves to +4 at 10th level.

description.summary: Dominate a Large or smaller living creature once per day.
description.fullText: At 7th level, the mindbender can dominate a single Large or smaller living creature within 100 feet as dominate monster once per day. A Will save negates the effect and the duration is 24 hours.

description.summary: Make one dominate effect permanent.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the mindbender can choose to make the duration of dominate permanent on one target at a time. If a new permanent thrall is created, the previous thrall is freed.

description.summary: A bardic music specialist who learns primal songs beyond ordinary bardic performance.
description.flavor: Seekers of the song wield primal music in ways that amaze even skilled bards, transforming performance into elemental power, healing, dispelling, and planar banishment.
description.role: Bardic music prestige class with no spellcasting progression, new seeker music effects, refrains, and stacking daily music uses.

special: Must have heard seeker music from another seeker of the song.

description.summary: Gain insight benefits while using bardic music, seeker music, or similar music.
description.fullText: While using bardic music, seeker music, or a similar ability, the seeker gains a +2 insight bonus to AC. At 4th level, she also gains a +2 insight bonus on saving throws. At 7th level, she also gains damage reduction 2/-. At 10th level, she also acts as though affected by freedom of movement.

description.summary: Use bardic music uses to activate seeker music and refrains.
description.fullText: Seeker music follows bardic music rules. Each seeker music effect costs one daily bardic music use to activate, seeker levels stack with bard levels to determine daily uses, and a refrain can be activated as a swift action by expending another daily use while maintaining the original music.

description.summary: Grant fire resistance to allies and unleash a cone of fire as a refrain.
description.fullText: At 1st level, a seeker with at least 14 Perform ranks can use Burning Melody. Allies within 30 feet who can hear the music gain fire resistance 15. The refrain creates a 30-foot cone of fire dealing 6d6 fire damage, Reflex half with DC 10 + Perform ranks.

description.summary: Activate and maintain two music abilities together.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the seeker can combine two types of bardic music or seeker music, activating both with the same standard action and maintaining concentration on both with one standard action each round.

description.summary: Damage constructs with a burst of primal music.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, a seeker with at least 15 Perform ranks can expend a bardic music use and make a Perform check to deal 1d8 damage per seeker level to all constructs within a 30-foot burst. There is no saving throw.

description.summary: Grant cold resistance and unleash a freezing line as a refrain.
description.fullText: At 3rd level, a seeker with at least 16 Perform ranks can grant allies within 30 feet cold resistance 15. The refrain creates a 60-foot line dealing 10d6 cold damage and fatigue, Fortitude half and negates fatigue with DC 10 + Perform ranks.

description.summary: Protect allies from poison and disease and heal with a refrain.
description.fullText: At 4th level, a seeker with at least 17 Perform ranks can grant allies within 30 feet immunity to poison and disease. The refrain heals a touched living creature for an amount equal to the Perform check result, up to its normal maximum hit points.

description.summary: Begin a new music effect as a swift action while one music effect is active.
description.fullText: At 5th level, the seeker can begin a new bardic music or seeker music effect as a swift action if exactly one bardic music or seeker music ability is already active.

description.summary: Grant electricity resistance and arc electrical rays as a refrain.
description.fullText: At 5th level, a seeker with at least 18 Perform ranks can grant allies within 30 feet electricity resistance 15. The refrain fires an initial 20-foot ranged touch ray dealing 10d6 electricity damage, then arcs to secondary foes within 20 feet of the first target; secondary rays deal half damage.

description.summary: Disrupt spellcasting and dispel magic as a refrain.
description.fullText: At 6th level, a seeker with at least 19 Perform ranks can force creatures that hear the hymn to make Concentration checks opposed by the seeker's Perform check to cast spells. The refrain works like area dispel magic centered on the seeker, with optional exclusion of the seeker and allies.

description.summary: Grant acid resistance and fire a lingering acid ray as a refrain.
description.fullText: At 7th level, a seeker with at least 20 Perform ranks can grant allies within 30 feet acid resistance 15. The refrain fires a 60-foot ranged touch ray dealing 10d6 acid damage and another 10d6 acid damage 1 round later.

description.summary: Teleport a short distance as a dimension door-like effect.
description.fullText: At 8th level, a seeker with at least 21 Perform ranks can expend a use of bardic music or seeker music to transport herself to any spot within 25 feet + 5 feet per level. This otherwise functions as dimension door, except she cannot bring other creatures.

mechanics.cost: one bardic music or seeker music use

description.summary: Grant sonic resistance, suppress nearby music abilities, and fire a sonic ray as a refrain.
description.fullText: At 9th level, a seeker with at least 22 Perform ranks can grant allies within 30 feet sonic resistance 15. While singing, other creatures within 30 feet must beat the seeker's Perform check with their own Perform check to use bardic music, seeker music, or similar abilities. The refrain fires a 60-foot ranged touch ray dealing 15d6 sonic damage.

description.summary: Send extraplanar creatures back to their home planes.
description.fullText: At 10th level, a seeker with at least 23 Perform ranks can spend music uses to affect extraplanar creatures within 60 feet. Affected creatures must make a Will save against DC 10 + Perform ranks, with a bonus equal to Hit Dice; those that fail are sent to their home plane.

description.summary: A bardic arcane caster who abandons ordinary bardic music progression to master high-level arcane harmony.
description.flavor: Sublime chords hear the music underlying magic itself, turning performance into access to powerful arcane spells and cosmic songs.
description.role: High-level spontaneous arcane caster with bardic knowledge/music interaction and unique bardic music powers.

requirements.spells: Able to cast 3rd-level arcane spells.

description.summary: Gain spontaneous arcane spellcasting from 4th through 9th level spells.
description.fullText: A sublime chord casts a small number of spontaneous arcane spells of 4th level and higher. Spells are chosen from the sorcerer/wizard and bard spell lists, using the bard version when a spell appears on both lists at different levels. Spellcasting is Charisma-based.

description.summary: Avoid arcane spell failure for sublime chord spells in light armor.
description.fullText: As with bard spells, the somatic components for sublime chord spells do not incur arcane spell failure chance if the sublime chord wears light armor.

description.summary: Add sublime chord levels to bardic knowledge checks.
description.fullText: The sublime chord adds her sublime chord class level to bardic knowledge checks.

description.summary: Gain new sublime chord songs and partial stacking for bardic music uses.
description.fullText: Sublime chord songs expend daily bardic music uses and function like bardic music effects. The sublime chord adds one-half class level, rounded down, to bard level to determine daily uses of bardic music.

mechanics.dailyUsesStacking: one-half sublime chord level, rounded down

description.summary: Spend bardic music to increase the caster level of the next spell.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, as a move action, the sublime chord can spend a bardic music use to prepare a spell with song. The next spell cast by the end of her next turn gains a caster level bonus based on the Perform check: +0 for 9 or lower, +1 for 10–19, +2 for 20–29, and +4 for 30 or higher.

description.summary: Freeze a creature in a timeless state.
description.fullText: At 6th level, as a standard action, the sublime chord can envelop a creature within 60 feet in timelessness. A Will save negates. On failure, the target can take no actions and cannot be affected by outside forces while the performance is maintained, up to 1 minute per level.

mechanics.duration: concentration, up to 1 minute per level

description.summary: Create a damaging burst of cosmic fire.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the sublime chord can spend two bardic music uses to create a 20-foot-radius spread of fire anywhere within 100 feet and line of effect. Creatures in the area take damage equal to the sublime chord's Perform check result, Reflex half.

description.summary: A divine spellcaster who deepens communion with a deity and gains domains and divine protections.
description.flavor: Contemplatives withdraw from worldly concerns to become vessels of divine insight and power.
description.role: Full divine casting prestige class with bonus domains, immunities, and mystical self-enhancement.

requirements.spells: Able to cast 1st-level divine spells.
requirements.special: Must have direct contact with a representative of the character's deity.

description.summary: Advance an existing divine spellcasting class at every level.
description.fullText: At each contemplative level, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if gaining a level in an existing divine spellcasting class.

description.summary: Gain additional clerical domains.
description.fullText: The contemplative gains an additional domain granted by the character's deity. A second bonus domain is gained at 6th level.

description.summary: Gain immunity to disease.
description.fullText: The contemplative is immune to all diseases, including supernatural and magical diseases.

description.summary: Gain a second chance against enchantments.
description.fullText: If the contemplative fails a saving throw against an enchantment spell or effect, the character can attempt the save again 1 round later.

description.summary: Heal damage through divine contemplation.
description.fullText: The contemplative can cure hit point damage to herself as a supernatural ability, drawing on a daily pool based on class level.

description.summary: Gain resistance to poison.
description.fullText: The contemplative gains immunity to poison as divine power purifies the body.

description.summary: Gain spell resistance.
description.fullText: The contemplative gains spell resistance as the soul becomes suffused with divine power.

description.summary: No longer suffer the physical penalties of aging.
description.fullText: The contemplative no longer takes ability score penalties for aging and cannot be magically aged, though existing penalties remain and bonuses still accrue.

description.summary: Become united with the divine.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the contemplative achieves a mystic union with the divine, gaining an outsider-like spiritual transformation appropriate to the deity and faith.

reviewNotes: Confirm late-level contemplative feature names/effects against primary source during in-app review.

description.summary: A devoted divine spellcaster who becomes an emissary of a patron deity.
description.flavor: Divine disciples channel a patron deity's power more directly than ordinary priests.
description.role: Five-level divine caster prestige class with a bonus domain, outsider interaction, and sacred defenses.
description.sourceText: Forgotten Realms Campaign Setting / Player's Guide to Faerûn

requirements.spells: Able to cast 4th-level divine spells.
requirements.special: Must be dedicated to a patron deity.

description.summary: Advance an existing divine spellcasting class at every level.
description.fullText: At each divine disciple level, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if gaining a level in an existing divine spellcasting class.

description.summary: Gain access to another domain.
description.fullText: At 1st level, the divine disciple gains access to an additional domain offered by the character's patron deity.

description.summary: Communicate with aligned or deity-serving outsiders.
description.fullText: The divine disciple can communicate telepathically with outsiders that serve the character's deity or share the character's alignment, and gains a bonus on Charisma-based checks when dealing with them.

mechanics.telepathyWith: outsiders serving patron deity or sharing alignment

description.summary: Gain a bonus against divine and outsider powers.
description.fullText: The divine disciple gains a sacred bonus on saving throws against divine spells and against spell-like or supernatural abilities of outsiders. The bonus improves at 4th level.

description.summary: Bestow spells on another creature.
description.fullText: The divine disciple can use imbue with spell ability as a spell-like ability, allowing the disciple to grant limited spellcasting ability to another creature.

description.summary: Achieve a divine transformation.
description.fullText: At 5th level, the divine disciple undergoes a divine transformation, becoming more closely tied to the patron deity and gaining an outsider-like spiritual nature.

reviewNotes: Divine Disciple is not a Complete Divine class; retained in this batch from requested list. Confirm 5th-level transformation wording/effects in app review.

description.summary: A divine warrior-caster devoted to protection, healing, and martial service.
description.flavor: Hospitalers defend the helpless and bring divine healing to those under their care.
description.role: Full-BAB divine class with spellcasting advancement, lay on hands, domains, and bonus martial feats.

requirements.alignment: LG, NG, LN, N, LE, or NE
requirements.spells: Able to cast 1st-level divine spells.

description.summary: Advance divine spellcasting at every hospitaler level.
description.fullText: At each hospitaler level, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if gaining a level in an existing divine spellcasting class.

description.summary: Cast as a cleric and gain two hospitaler domains.
description.fullText: The hospitaler casts spells as a cleric and gains two domains from the hospitaler domain options. These domains supersede any current domains as described by the class.

description.summary: Heal others with a daily pool.
description.fullText: The hospitaler can heal a total number of hit points per day equal to class level × Charisma modifier. This stacks with other lay on hands abilities.

description.summary: Gain bonus feats selected from the fighter bonus feat list.
description.fullText: The hospitaler gains bonus fighter feats over the course of the class.

description.summary: Use remove disease a limited number of times per week.
description.fullText: The hospitaler gains the ability to remove disease as the class advances.

description.summary: Hospitaler levels stack for special mount benefits.
description.fullText: Hospitaler levels stack with paladin levels for purposes of determining a paladin special mount's abilities when applicable.

reviewNotes: Confirm Hospitaler domain replacement text, bonus feat levels, remove disease progression, and mount interaction against primary source.

description.summary: A divine spellcaster dedicated to expelling possessing spirits and evil supernatural forces.
description.flavor: Sacred exorcists are called to confront fiends, undead, and corrupting spirits wherever they hide.
description.role: Full divine casting prestige class with turning, anti-possession abilities, and anti-evil supernatural tools.

requirements.spells: Able to cast dismissal or dispel evil as a divine spell.
requirements.special: Must be sanctioned by a church or order to perform exorcisms.

description.summary: Advance an existing divine spellcasting class at every level.
description.fullText: At each sacred exorcist level, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if gaining a level in an existing divine spellcasting class.

description.summary: Gain the ability to turn undead.
description.fullText: At 1st level, the sacred exorcist can turn undead as a cleric. Sacred exorcist levels stack with other turning classes for turning checks and turning damage.

description.summary: Force possessing spirits out of a victim.
description.fullText: The sacred exorcist can attempt to force a possessing creature or spirit out of a victim, using divine authority against possession and similar control.

description.summary: Gain bonuses against possession and compulsion.
description.fullText: The sacred exorcist gains a sacred bonus on saving throws against possession and similar mental control. The bonus improves at higher levels.

description.summary: Detect evil at will.
description.fullText: The sacred exorcist can use detect evil at will as a spell-like ability.

description.summary: Gain bonuses against evil outsiders.
description.fullText: The sacred exorcist gains bonuses against evil outsiders and creatures involved in possession or corruption. The bonus improves with class level.

description.summary: Use dispel evil as a limited spell-like ability.
description.fullText: The sacred exorcist gains the ability to use dispel evil as a spell-like ability a limited number of times per week.

description.summary: Radiate holy power against evil influences.
description.fullText: The sacred exorcist radiates consecrated power that helps oppose evil creatures, possession, and undead within the exorcist's presence.

description.summary: Manifest powerful holy protection.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the sacred exorcist can manifest a powerful holy protective effect as the culmination of the class's anti-evil powers.

reviewNotes: Confirm Sacred Exorcist exact feature names, chosen foe scaling, dispel evil uses, and 10th-level capstone against primary source during in-app review.

description.summary: A divine summoning specialist who improves planar ally and summoning magic.
description.flavor: Thaumaturgists bargain with extraplanar powers and strengthen their conjured servants.
description.role: Divine caster advancement class focused on summoning, planar allies, and an extraplanar cohort.

requirements.spells: Able to cast lesser planar ally.

description.summary: Advance an existing divine spellcasting class at every thaumaturgist level.
description.fullText: At each thaumaturgist level, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if also gaining a level in an existing divine spellcasting class.

description.summary: Call planar allies at reduced payment.
description.fullText: When the thaumaturgist casts a planar ally spell, the called creature requires a reduced payment for its service.

description.summary: Gain Augment Summoning as a bonus feat.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the thaumaturgist gains Augment Summoning as a bonus feat.

description.summary: Summoning spells last longer.
description.fullText: At 3rd level, all conjuration summoning spells cast by the thaumaturgist have their durations doubled as if affected by Extend Spell, without changing spell level.

description.summary: Prepare a summoning spell to trigger later.
description.fullText: At 4th level, the thaumaturgist can prepare a summoning or calling spell to take effect under a stated condition, functioning as a contingency-like class ability with limits.

description.summary: Gain a called extraplanar cohort.
description.fullText: At 5th level, the thaumaturgist gains a planar cohort. The cohort is acquired through planar ally magic and follows the class's cohort limitations.

description.summary: A Pelorite divine caster who channels sunlight, healing, and positive energy.
description.flavor: Radiant servants of Pelor are champions of light and healing who burn away undead corruption.
description.role: Full divine caster progression class with enhanced healing, turning, and anti-undead abilities.

requirements.spells: Able to cast 3rd-level divine spells and access the Sun domain.

description.summary: Advance an existing divine spellcasting class at every level.
description.fullText: At each radiant servant of Pelor level, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if gaining a level in an existing divine spellcasting class.

description.summary: Gain additional greater turning uses.
description.fullText: The radiant servant gains additional daily uses of greater turning through the Sun domain, using radiant servant levels as applicable.

description.summary: Emit holy light.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the radiant servant can radiate bright light that harms or hinders undead as described by the class ability.

description.summary: Gain immunity to disease.
description.fullText: At 3rd level, the radiant servant becomes immune to all diseases, including supernatural and magical diseases.

description.summary: Empower healing domain spells.
description.fullText: At 4th level, healing spells from the Healing domain are empowered as if affected by Empower Spell, without using a higher-level spell slot.

description.summary: Ward nearby allies against negative energy and death effects.
description.fullText: At 5th level, the radiant servant projects a protective aura that helps allies resist negative energy and death effects.

description.summary: Maximize healing domain spells.
description.fullText: At 6th level, healing spells from the Healing domain are maximized as if affected by Maximize Spell, without using a higher-level spell slot.

description.summary: Release positive energy against undead.
description.fullText: At 8th level, the radiant servant can expend turning energy to create a burst of positive energy that damages undead and heals living creatures as specified by the class.

description.summary: Healing domain spells heal the maximum possible amount.
description.fullText: At 10th level, healing spells from the Healing domain are treated as maximized and empowered as specified by the class feature.

description.summary: A divine storm warrior who channels lightning and thunder through weapons.
description.flavor: Stormlords devote themselves to the fury of the skies, becoming warriors of wind, thunder, and lightning.
description.role: Divine martial prestige class with storm-themed weapon enhancements and electricity defenses.

requirements.spells: Able to cast 3rd-level divine spells.
special: Must worship a storm deity or have similar storm devotion

description.summary: Move through storms and difficult weather more easily.
description.fullText: The stormlord gains resilience and mobility in stormy conditions, including protection from many penalties caused by high winds and storms.

description.summary: Add electricity damage to thrown or spear-like weapons.
description.fullText: The stormlord's favored storm weapons gain electricity damage. The effect improves as class level increases.

description.summary: Gain resistance to electricity.
description.fullText: The stormlord gains resistance to electricity that improves with class level.

description.summary: Weapons crack with thunder on critical hits.
description.fullText: The stormlord's storm weapons gain a thundering quality or similar sonic critical effect as described by the class.

description.summary: Travel through storms.
description.fullText: At higher level, the stormlord can ride storm winds or otherwise travel through severe weather using supernatural storm power.

description.summary: Become immune to electricity.
description.fullText: The stormlord eventually becomes immune to electricity.

description.summary: Become a living avatar of storm power.
description.fullText: At 10th level, the stormlord gains a powerful storm-themed transformation or capstone as defined by the class.

reviewNotes: Stormlord class features vary by exact Complete Divine table wording; review weapon enhancement and storm travel details in app.

description.summary: A contemplative caster who perceives and manipulates the mystical void.
description.flavor: Void disciples seek the emptiness underlying all things, drawing insight and supernatural perception from nothingness.
description.role: Unusual spellcasting prestige class with void perception and supernatural insight abilities.

requirements.spells: Able to cast divination spells.
special: Must pursue training in void mysteries

description.summary: Advance an existing spellcasting class at most levels.
description.fullText: Void disciple levels advance spellcasting at the levels indicated by the class progression.

description.summary: Extend senses through the void.
description.fullText: The void disciple can extend perception through the void to observe distant locations. Range and potency improve with level.

description.summary: Grant insight through the void.
description.fullText: The void disciple can momentarily reveal hidden truth, granting a target a bonus or special insight as described by the class.

description.summary: Release void energy.
description.fullText: The void disciple can release or manipulate void energy as a supernatural class ability.

description.summary: Suppress or interfere with a creature through the void.
description.fullText: The void disciple gains the ability to suppress or influence a creature through void manipulation.

description.summary: Become partly one with the void.
description.fullText: At the height of the class, the void disciple gains a void-themed transformation or defensive state.

reviewNotes: Void Disciple uses unusually specific supernatural abilities; confirm exact level table and mechanics from sourcebook before marking mechanics complete.

description.summary: A lawful divine agent who exposes corruption, deception, and supernatural infiltration.
description.flavor: Church inquisitors protect their faith from corruption within and threats disguised as truth.
description.role: Full divine casting advancement prestige class with truth detection, enchantment immunities, and investigative divine powers.

requirements.spells: Able to cast dispel magic as a divine spell.
special: Must be a member of a lawful good church or religious order and must have uncovered corruption within it.

description.summary: Advance an existing divine spellcasting class at each class level.
description.fullText: At each church inquisitor level, the character gains new spells per day and an increase in caster level as if gaining a level in an existing divine spellcasting class.

description.summary: Detect evil at will.
description.fullText: The church inquisitor can use detect evil at will as a spell-like ability.

description.summary: Gain the Inquisition domain.
description.fullText: The church inquisitor gains access to the Inquisition domain and its granted power.

description.summary: Become immune to charm effects.
description.fullText: The church inquisitor becomes immune to enchantment charm spells and effects.

description.summary: Gain a bonus or special check against illusions.
description.fullText: The church inquisitor gains a class ability that helps pierce illusions and deceptive magic.

description.summary: Gain superior ability to detect disguises and hidden identities.
description.fullText: The church inquisitor gains a class ability that helps detect mundane or magical disguises and concealed identities.

description.summary: Become immune to compulsion effects.
description.fullText: The church inquisitor becomes immune to enchantment compulsion spells and effects.

description.summary: Force a shapechanged creature toward its true form.
description.fullText: The church inquisitor gains the ability to challenge or suppress shapechanging and reveal a creature's true form.

description.summary: Discern lies as a spell-like ability.
description.fullText: The church inquisitor can discern lies as a spell-like ability with limited daily uses.

description.summary: Resist or ignore possession and mental control.
description.fullText: The church inquisitor gains immunity or strong protection against possession and similar controlling effects.

description.summary: Compel or reveal truth through divine insight.
description.fullText: The church inquisitor gains a high-level truth-revealing ability suitable for exposing corruption and deception.

reviewNotes: Several mid/high-level inquisitor abilities should be verified against the primary class table/text in-app.

description.summary: A church-sanctioned hunter who tracks and destroys enemies of the faith.
description.flavor: Consecrated harriers pursue marked enemies relentlessly, empowered by scripture and divine mandate.
description.role: Divine tracking prestige class with quarry bonuses, limited casting progression, and pursuit abilities.

special: Must successfully track down and destroy a target selected by the character's church.

description.summary: Gain bonuses against a church-assigned target.
description.fullText: The consecrated harrier gains sacred bonuses on certain skill checks and attack/damage rolls against a church-assigned target.

description.summary: Detect chaos at will.
description.fullText: The consecrated harrier can use detect chaos at will as a spell-like ability.

description.summary: Advance divine spellcasting at selected levels.
description.fullText: The consecrated harrier gains spellcasting advancement at the levels indicated by the class table, advancing an existing divine spellcasting class.

description.summary: Gain a bonus against illusions.
description.fullText: The consecrated harrier gains a bonus on saving throws against illusion effects.

description.summary: Summon or call a divine hunting aid.
description.fullText: The consecrated harrier gains a divinely empowered hunting ability or hound-themed aid as described by the class.

description.summary: Improve pursuit of the assigned target.
description.fullText: The consecrated harrier gains increasing bonuses or abilities related to pursuing the church-assigned quarry.

description.summary: Locate the assigned quarry through divine power.
description.fullText: At high level, the consecrated harrier can use a powerful divination effect to locate the assigned target.

reviewNotes: Spellcasting level pattern and later pursuit abilities should be checked against primary source table.

description.summary: A servant of Kelemvor who hunts undead and enforces the proper passage of souls.
description.flavor: Doomguides oppose undeath as a violation of natural death and divine judgment.
description.role: Short undead-hunter prestige class with turning and anti-undead abilities.

requirements.alignment: Usually lawful neutral or compatible with Kelemvor's clergy
special: Must serve Kelemvor or the appropriate death faith.

description.summary: Improve effective level for turning undead.
description.fullText: Doomguide levels improve or stack with cleric levels for turning undead as described by the class.

description.summary: Gain additional turning uses.
description.fullText: The doomguide gains additional uses or improved capability with turn undead.

description.summary: Gain bonuses against undead abilities.
description.fullText: The doomguide gains a scaling sacred or class bonus against attacks and abilities of undead creatures.

description.summary: Channel death against undead or living foes.
description.fullText: The doomguide gains a death-themed touch or channeling ability associated with Kelemvor's judgment of the undead.

description.summary: Prevent undead from returning easily.
description.fullText: The doomguide gains a capstone or high-level ability that helps destroy undead permanently or prevent their return.

reviewNotes: Doomguide is not a Complete Divine class; primary Forgotten Realms source mechanics should be verified in-app/source before marking complete.

description.summary: A divine assassin of Kossuth who combines stealth, death attacks, poison, and black fire.
description.flavor: Black flame zealots are ruthless temple killers who wield sacred fire as a weapon of execution.
description.role: Sneak-attack divine prestige class with death attack, poison use, partial divine casting, and fire-themed abilities.

requirements.spells: Able to cast 2nd-level divine spells.
special: Must worship Kossuth or meet the class's faith requirement.

description.summary: Study a target and deliver a deadly sneak attack.
description.fullText: The black flame zealot gains a death attack similar to the assassin, allowing a studied melee sneak attack to kill or otherwise incapacitate a target.

description.summary: Become immune to fear.
description.fullText: The black flame zealot becomes immune to magical and mundane fear effects.

description.summary: Advance existing divine spellcasting at selected levels.
description.fullText: The black flame zealot gains new spells per day and caster level as if gaining a level in an existing divine spellcasting class at the levels indicated by the class progression.

description.summary: Use poison without risking accidental self-poisoning.
description.fullText: The black flame zealot is trained in the use of poison and does not risk accidentally poisoning himself when applying poison to a blade.

description.summary: Gain additional sneak attack damage.
description.fullText: The black flame zealot gains additional sneak attack damage at specific levels. This damage stacks with sneak attack from other sources.

description.summary: Channel black fire through attacks or divine wrath.
description.fullText: The black flame zealot gains the signature black flame ability, channeling supernatural fire associated with the deity's wrath.

description.summary: Burn enemies with black divine fire.
description.fullText: At higher levels, the black flame zealot can immolate foes with black flame or enhance attacks with destructive divine fire.

description.summary: Improve the lethality of death attack.
description.fullText: The black flame zealot's death attack becomes more dangerous as class level increases.

reviewNotes: Black flame feature details and exact death attack improvement should be verified against source text/table.

description.role: Martial
