description.summary: A warrior who channels rage into uncontrollable destructive frenzy.
description.flavor: Frenzied berserkers surrender restraint for terrifying battlefield power.
description.role: High-risk melee striker with frenzy, enhanced Power Attack, and death-defying rage mechanics.

description.summary: Enter a powerful but dangerous frenzy.
description.fullText: The frenzied berserker can enter a frenzy, gaining a large Strength bonus and an extra attack while risking loss of control. Uses per day increase by level.

description.summary: Increase the damage return from Power Attack.
description.fullText: The frenzied berserker improves Power Attack damage, with a stronger multiplier for two-handed weapons as the class advances.

description.summary: Keep fighting while below dying thresholds.
description.fullText: At higher level, the frenzied berserker can continue fighting while in frenzy even when damage would normally render the character dying or dead until the frenzy ends.

description.summary: Improve frenzy benefits.
description.fullText: The frenzied berserker gains an improved form of frenzy with increased offensive power.

description.summary: Drive allies into frenzy.
description.fullText: The frenzied berserker can inspire frenzy in willing allies or nearby creatures as described by the class feature.

reviewNotes: Exact frenzy compulsion, save DCs, fatigue/exhaustion, and Power Attack multiplier text should be reviewed in-app against Complete Warrior.

description.summary: A dagger and feint specialist who turns deception into lethal precision.
description.flavor: Invisible blades are knife fighters whose hands move faster than the eye can follow.
description.role: Short melee precision class focused on feinting, bleeding wounds, and defense while lightly equipped.

feats: Weapon Focus (dagger, kukri, or punching dagger)

description.summary: Add Intelligence bonus to AC while fighting lightly.
description.fullText: The invisible blade adds Intelligence bonus to Armor Class when unarmored or lightly equipped under the class restrictions, limited by class level or feature wording.

description.summary: Cause sneak attacks to bleed.
description.fullText: The invisible blade can forgo sneak attack dice to cause bleeding wounds that deal ongoing damage.

description.summary: Feint more quickly in combat.
description.fullText: The invisible blade gains improved action economy when using Bluff to feint in combat.

description.summary: Improve combat feint reliability.
description.fullText: The invisible blade gains additional benefits or reliability with feinting in combat as represented by the class feature progression.

reviewNotes: Invisible Blade requirements and feature wording changed in errata/discussion; review against the chosen Complete Warrior source/errata policy.

description.summary: A specialist in thrown weapons and precision throwing tricks.
description.flavor: Master throwers turn knives, axes, and other thrown weapons into a deadly art.
description.role: Short martial prestige class built around selectable thrown weapon tricks and ranged precision.

description.summary: Gain Quick Draw as a bonus feat.
description.fullText: The master thrower gains Quick Draw as a bonus feat if the character does not already have it.

description.summary: Learn special thrown weapon tricks.
description.fullText: The master thrower learns a thrown weapon trick at multiple class levels, chosen from the master thrower trick list.

description.summary: Avoid damage on successful Reflex saves.
description.fullText: At 2nd level, the master thrower gains evasion.

description.summary: Improve thrown weapon criticals.
description.fullText: The master thrower gains improved critical performance or a related thrown weapon benefit as described by the class progression.

description.summary: Target vulnerable points with thrown weapons.
description.fullText: The master thrower can use a specialized trick or feature to target weak spots with thrown weapons.

reviewNotes: Thrown weapon trick list should be modeled later as selectable subfeatures/options.

description.summary: A martial anti-mage trained to resist and destroy spellcasters.
description.flavor: Occult slayers distrust magic and train to survive the tricks of spellcasters.
description.role: Short martial prestige class with anti-magic defenses and spellcaster-hunting tools.

description.summary: Gain bonuses on saves against spells and spell-like abilities.
description.fullText: The occult slayer gains a scaling bonus on saving throws against spells and spell-like abilities.

description.summary: Bond with a weapon for anti-magic combat.
description.fullText: The occult slayer forms a bond with a chosen weapon, allowing class features to function through that weapon.

description.summary: Turn magic back through force of will.
description.fullText: The occult slayer can use a special defense against spells or spell-like abilities, potentially negating or redirecting magical effects.

description.summary: Deal extra damage against spellcasters or magical foes.
description.fullText: The occult slayer gains improved offensive capability against spellcasters or creatures using magic.

description.summary: Resist mind-affecting detection or control.
description.fullText: The occult slayer gains protection against mind-affecting magic and supernatural mental influence.

description.summary: Perceive magical auras.
description.fullText: The occult slayer can perceive magical auras or magical power around creatures and objects.

reviewNotes: Occult Slayer feature names/precise mechanics should be verified against Complete Warrior; current model is structurally valid for in-app review.
